The parliamentary law depart
ment of the WOman's club held
their last meeting Thursday at the
library, with Mrs. A. p. Fassett as
chairman and teacher


A parliamentary match was con-
Quoted, and questions asked includ-
ed the work accomplished during
the year. Mrs. o. A. Thompson and
Mrs. w. M. Gleason carried off the
trophies.


Mrs. Fassett will be retained as
teacher next year, as she has prov
ed herself to be an excellent parlia-
mentarian and imparts her knowl-
edge with ease and dexterity. The
same books will be resOrted to next
year. Much time will also be spent
on drill work.


& 4 &


Much interest is centered on the
benefit the B. S. Lawrence Stock
company will give Monday and
Tuesday evenings, May 80 and 81,
for the Woman's club building fund


THE Lost Trail" is western
play and will no doubt be a success
when handled by the local COM
pany.
